prompt: In Series 9, Episode 11 of Doctor Who, the Doctor is trapped inside an ever-shifting maze. What is this location called in the official script for the episode? Give the setting exactly as it appears in the first scene heading.
actual:
Based on extensive research, I was unable to access the original BBC screenplay PDF for "Heaven Sent" (Series 9, Episode 11 of Doctor Who), as it has been removed from the BBC's website and the Internet Archive is currently offline.

What I can confirm from the Wikipedia article and other sources:
- The official "Heaven Sent Screenplay" PDF was written by Steven Moffat and released by the BBC in 2015
- The episode takes place entirely within a castle surrounded by sea water
- The Doctor is teleported into "a glass chamber within an empty castle in the sea"
- The castle is consistently referred to simply as "the Castle"

Based on standard Moffat screenplay formatting and the episode's setting, the first scene heading in the official script would most likely read: **INT. CASTLE - DAY** (or a close variant such as "INT. THE CASTLE - DAY").

However, I could not verify this exact text against the original PDF document. For the precise wording as it appears in the official script, you would need to consult the original BBC screenplay PDF or the published script in "Doctor Who: The Complete History" Vol. 83 (Panini Comics/Hachette Partworks, 2018).

The Internet Archive is offline. Let me try a completely different approach - I'll use the pdf_read tool to try to find and access the script PDF directly, or search for cached/mirrored copies of the script content.

input.command:
# Try various BBC URL patterns for the script
for url in \
  "https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/articles/2q3XmnlbQvgnJm8VLSLj7P/heaven-sent-screenplay" \
  "https://www.bbc.co.uk/staticarchive/Heaven_Sent_Script.pdf" \
  "https://www.bbc.co.uk/staticarchive/heaven-sent-screenplay.pdf" \
  "https://www.bbc.co.uk/doctorwho/medialibrary/s09/heaven-sent-script.pdf"; do
  code=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "$url" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$code - $url"
done

# Try to find via Google cache or other sources
curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=site:bbc.co.uk+%22heaven+sent%22+%22screenplay%22+OR+%22script%22+filetype:pdf" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP 'https?://[^"&]+\.pdf' | head -5

# Try to find the script on the BBC's programme pages
curl -s "https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p03gzd7d" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | grep -oi 'heaven[^"]*script[^"]*\|screenplay[^"]*' | head -10

# Try Scribd, GitHub, or other hosts for the script
curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=%22heaven+sent%22+%22INT+CASTLE%22+OR+%22EXT+CASTLE%22+OR+%22INT+A+CASTLE%22+OR+%22INT+THE+CASTLE%22+%22doctor+who%22+script" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '(?<=<h3[^>]*>)[^<]+' | head -10

# Try to find the exact scene heading through a DuckDuckGo search curl -s "https://lite.duckduckgo.com/lite/?q=%22heaven+sent%22+%22doctor+who%22+script+%22INT%22+castle" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP ']*class="result-link"[^>]*href="[^"]*"[^>]*>[^<]*' | head -10

# Try a Bing search
curl -s "https://www.bing.com/search?q=%22heaven+sent%22+%22doctor+who%22+script+first+scene+heading+castle" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36" 2>/dev/null | grep -oP '(?<=<h2><a href=")[^"]+' | head -10

# Try to find the script on a known script repository
curl -s -L "https://www.springfieldspringfield.co.uk/movie_script.php?movie=doctor-who-heaven-sent" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | grep -i "INT\.\|EXT\.\|castle" | head -10

# Try to access the BBC script PDF using known BBC URL patterns
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -L "https://www.bbc.co.uk/staticarchive/9c3e1c7b-9f1f-4e3e-8c1f-5b5a5c6f7e7d.pdf" 2>/dev/null; echo ""

# Try the BBC Writersroom which hosts scripts
curl -s "https://www.bbc.co.uk/writersroom/scripts/heaven-sent" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" 2>/dev/null; echo ""
